title: Where to Purchase Bitcoin Cash (BCH): Full Guide
content: [Bitcoin](/bitcoin) Cash (BCH) remains a prominent asset for those interested in decentralized, high-throughput payment networks. As a veteran of the Proof-of-Work ecosystem, it continues to hold a market rank of #16 within the global crypto economy. | Metric | Details | | --- | --- | | Current Price | $454.81 | | 24H Change | -0.24% | | Market Rank | #16 | | Date | April 25, 2026 | ## Why Buy Bitcoin Cash? Bitcoin Cash was created to scale Bitcoin's capacity, allowing for faster and cheaper transactions. For users and merchants looking to utilize a blockchain for payments, BCH provides a established infrastructure compared to alternatives like [Litecoin](/litecoin) or privacy-focused coins like [Zcash](/zcash). ## Top Exchange Options Most major centralized exchanges list Bitcoin Cash due to its long-standing history and liquidity. Platforms such as Coinbase, Kraken, and Binance generally offer direct trading pairs for BCH against major fiat currencies (USD, EUR) and stablecoins. ## Purchase Tutorial 1. Choose a Reputable Exchange: Select a platform that supports BCH and meets your security standards. 2. Complete KYC: Ensure your identity verification is processed to enable fiat deposits. 3. Deposit Fiat: Use bank transfers or credit cards to fund your account. 4. Execute Trade: Navigate to the BCH trading pair and enter your buy order at the current market price of $454.81. 5. Confirm: Once the trade executes, the assets will appear in your exchange wallet. ## Securing Your Tokens Storing tokens on an exchange introduces counterparty risk. For long-term holdings, a hardware wallet (such as Ledger or Trezor) is widely considered the industry standard for security. Always ensure you control your private keys when holding meaningful amounts. ## Important Risks Bitcoin Cash carries a TokenRadar Risk Score of 6, designating it as a medium-risk asset. While it has survived significant market stress tests, including the 2018 hashwar, it remains subject to market volatility and technical competition from emerging protocols. Always perform thorough due diligence before committing capital. ## FAQ 1. Should I keep my BCH on an exchange? It is generally safer to move your holdings to a private, non-custodial hardware wallet. 2. Is it expensive to buy BCH? The cost is determined by current market price and exchange transaction fees. 3. How long does a purchase take? On most exchanges, the process from fiat deposit to holding the token takes only a few minutes. --- *Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. Always do your own research (DYOR).*
